name: Halifax
summary: Halifax is the biggest city in Atlantic Canada, built around one of the world's largest natural harbours. It is famous for its star-shaped hilltop fort, its booming noon cannon and its strong links to the Titanic.
funFacts: Soldiers have fired the noon gun on Citadel Hill almost every single day since 1857, and the boom echoes right across Halifax Harbour.
When the Titanic sank in 1912, rescue ships brought the lost to Halifax, and 121 people are buried together at Fairview Lawn Cemetery.
In 1917 a ship full of explosives blew up in the harbour. It was the biggest human-made blast on Earth before the atomic bomb.
The Old Town Clock at the foot of the Citadel has been ticking since 1803 and is one of the oldest working clocks in North America.
Halifax invented the donair, a messy spiced-beef wrap with sweet garlicky sauce, and made it the city's official food in 2015.
thingsToDo: Climb up to the star-shaped Halifax Citadel and cover your ears for the famous noon gun cannon firing.
Wander the colourful flowerbeds and Victorian bandstand at the leafy Halifax Public Gardens.
See real Titanic deckchairs and the Unknown Child's tiny shoes at the Maritime Museum of the Atlantic.
Drive out to Peggy's Cove to scramble across pink granite boulders around Canada's most photographed lighthouse.
Stroll the Halifax Waterfront Boardwalk for ice cream, buskers and a giant wooden wave to climb on.
Look up at the Old Town Clock, an unusual three-tiered wooden clock tower that has chimed since 1803.

wildlife.name: Humpback Whale
wildlife.description: Giant ocean animal that swims past Nova Scotia each summer to feed in cool Atlantic waters.

wildlife.name: Harbour Seal
wildlife.description: Chubby spotted swimmer often spotted bobbing its head out of Halifax Harbour.

wildlife.name: Atlantic Puffin
wildlife.description: Small black-and-white seabird with a brightly striped beak, sometimes called a sea parrot.

wildlife.name: Bald Eagle
wildlife.description: Huge brown eagle with a snowy white head that soars over Nova Scotia's coast.

localWord.meaning: Hello, in Mi'kmaw, the language of Nova Scotia's First People.

images.alt: Sunset glowing pink and gold over Halifax Harbour with the city skyline reflected in the calm water.
The star-shaped stone walls of the historic Halifax Citadel fortress sitting on top of a green hill.
The colourful Victorian gates at the entrance of the leafy Halifax Public Gardens.
The Maritime Museum of the Atlantic on the Halifax boardwalk, with a tall ship moored alongside.
The bright red and white Peggy's Cove lighthouse standing on smooth granite rocks beside the blue Atlantic Ocean.
The white three-tiered Old Town Clock tower on the slope of Citadel Hill in Halifax.
A humpback whale breaching out of the ocean.
A spotted harbour seal resting on a rock by the sea.
An Atlantic puffin standing on a grassy clifftop, showing its bright orange and yellow striped beak.
A bald eagle in flight, with a snowy white head and big brown wings spread wide.
